subject: Review C-DRP2.9: unnamed
body:
## Claim Review Request: C-DRP2.9
**Paper:** DRP-2 — Testability as a Relational Property: Epistemological Consequences of the Upstream Measurement Principle
**Claim:** unnamed
**Type:** F

### Statement
AI systems trained on DRS-formatted corpora outperform AI systems trained on standard-format prose at the task of classifying falsification predicates as vacuous or non-vacuous, and this gap does not close with increased scale of prose training.

### Falsification Predicate
**FALSIFIED_IF:** (accuracy_DRS_trained - accuracy_prose_trained) <= 0.10
**WHERE:** [{'name': 'accuracy_DRS_trained', 'type': 'scalar', 'units': 'dimensionless', 'source': 'accuracy of system trained on DRS-formatted corpus on benchmark defined in §4.2.1'}, {'name': 'accuracy_prose_trained', 'type': 'scalar', 'units': 'dimensionless', 'source': 'accuracy of system trained on matched prose corpus (same domain, same claim content, different format) on same §4.2.1 benchmark'}]
**EVALUATION:** train two matched systems on DRS-formatted vs prose corpora respectively; evaluate both on §4.2.1 benchmark; compute accuracy gap; test at 1x, 2x, 4x prose training data to verify gap persists; finite
**BOUNDARY:** gap = 0.10 → FALSIFIED (inclusive; 10pp = minimum meaningful structural advantage)
**CONTEXT:** C-DRP2.9 · PRIOR PREDICTION: DRS format encodes O-from-P structure; prose format does not; gap expected > 0.20 · scale robustness test required

### Requested
1. Verify the falsification predicate is logically consistent
2. Check math against known results (Strogatz, Kuznetsov, etc.)
3. Identify counterexamples or edge cases missed
4. Cross-reference against published literature
5. Provide verdict with confidence and reasoning
